steps.title: Read the doubling structure
steps.content: The model is $$f(t)=48{,}000\cdot 2^{t/820}.$$ The starting amount is $48{,}000$, and the power of $2$ controls how many doublings happen.

steps.title: Set one doubling
steps.content: For the population to double once, the multiplier must be $2^1$. So set the exponent equal to $1$: $$\frac{t}{820}=1.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $820$: $t=820.$ This matches choice B. The distractors confuse that time with the base, two doubling periods, or the initial population.
